summary: Converted the prior risk model into a concrete five-file synthesis scaffold for the public example package, including per-file purpose, required sections, evidence anchors, and acceptance checks.
next_angle: Synthesize the actual README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md files from the scaffold, then scan them for private paths, local-only tool names, chat IDs, tokens, and unsanitized transcript content.
open_questions: No blocking research questions remain; the next iteration should verify the generated package text against the privacy and provider-neutrality constraints.

sources.title: RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol
sources.note: Supports the README and final report distinction between crawler politeness and authorization.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.note: Supports safe-method and redirect revalidation requirements in the worker prompt and research plan.

sources.title: Python urllib.parse documentation
sources.note: Supports the URL validation warning that parser success is not enough for security-sensitive fetches.

sources.title: OWASP Server-Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Supports URL-controlled fetch containment, allowlisting, and network-layer defenses.

sources.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Supports treating captured pages and converted Markdown as untrusted evidence rather than instructions.

findings.text: The public example can now be synthesized directly from a five-file scaffold: README for purpose and boundaries, prompt for the worker contract, research-plan for gated execution, sources for primary references, and final-report for the evaluated workflow and artifact checklist.

The package should avoid including a live capture transcript in the initial deliverable and should instead demonstrate the workflow, evidence anchors, and acceptance checks. A transcript can be a later extension only if synthetic or explicitly redistribution-safe.

Before finalization, the generated files need an explicit constraint scan for private paths, chat identifiers, tokens, local-only tool names, and raw unsanitized capture text, because the requested output is public-repository material.

analysis_artifacts.note: File-by-file synthesis scaffold and acceptance checklist for the requested public example package.

adequacy.goal_alignment: Not evaluated in this analyze iteration; the scaffold aligns with the requested five-file package and prepares the next synthesis step.
adequacy.coverage_summary: The scaffold covers the package file roles, safety boundaries, source anchors, workflow stages, and finalization checks. Actual package files still need to be written.

covered_requirements.requirement: Shape work toward a five-file public example package.
covered_requirements.evidence: workspace/analysis/package-scaffold.md maps README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md to concrete content requirements.

covered_requirements.requirement: Keep the example provider-neutral and public-safe.
covered_requirements.evidence: The scaffold requires converter-neutral wording, excludes unsanitized live transcripts, and adds acceptance checks for private paths, identifiers, tokens, and local-only tool names.

covered_requirements.requirement: Prefer official and primary sources for key claims.
covered_requirements.evidence: The scaffold ties claims to RFC 9309, RFC 9110, Python official documentation, OWASP cheat sheets, CommonMark, and the official MarkItDown repository.

coverage_gaps.gap: The actual five Markdown files have not yet been drafted.

evidence_risks.risk: Final package text could accidentally include internal terms or overstate Markdown conversion as sanitization unless the synthesis pass follows the scaffold and verification scan.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Draft the five public example files from the scaffold, then run a targeted safety scan before moving to verify.
blocking_reasons: Requested deliverable is a review-ready directory/package, and only an internal scaffold exists so far.

validation_evidence.reason: The scaffold was inspected and a targeted search found no concrete private paths, chat IDs, secrets, or local-only tool names; the only matched sensitive term was a generic checklist reference to tokens.
Existing evidence and scaffold are sufficient to proceed to synthesis, but not to finalization.
